description: Sample couple and parent-child conversation transcripts for MindShift tone analysis testing

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: You said you'd take care of the dishes three hours ago. They're still sitting there.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I was going to get to them. You don't have to remind me every five minutes.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Every five minutes? I asked you once. Three hours ago. That's the problem — you don't even hear me.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Here we go again. Everything I do is wrong.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: I'm not saying everything is wrong. I just need you to follow through when you say you'll do something.

conversations.scenario: Couple reconnecting after a disagreement about finances

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Hey, I've been thinking about what you said last night about the budget. You had some good points.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Really? Because it felt like you just shut down when I brought it up.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I know. I did shut down. I get overwhelmed when money comes up because it makes me feel like I'm not providing enough.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: I didn't realize that's what was going on. I'm not saying you're not providing — I just want us to plan together.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: That makes sense. Can we sit down this weekend and look at everything together?

conversations.scenario: Parent setting boundaries with teenager about screen time

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Riley, it's 11 PM on a school night. You need to put the phone away.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: All my friends are still online. You're the only parent who does this.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I get that it feels unfair. But sleep matters, and tomorrow you have that math test.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Fine. But can I at least say goodnight to Avery?

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Two minutes, then phone on the charger in the kitchen. Deal?

conversations.scenario: One partner stonewalling during discussion about visiting in-laws

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: My mom asked if we're coming for Easter. We need to give her an answer.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Mm-hmm.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Drew. Can you actually talk to me about this? I feel like I'm talking to a wall.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: What do you want me to say? You already know I don't want to go. But you're going to make us go anyway.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I don't want to make you do anything. But I need you to tell me what you're actually feeling instead of shutting down.

conversations.scenario: Parent comforting child after a bad day at school

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Nobody picked me for their team at recess today. Again.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Oh buddy, that sounds really painful. Come sit with me.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: I hate recess. I just want to stay inside and read.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: It makes sense you'd want to avoid something that hurts. Being left out is one of the worst feelings.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Do you think there's something wrong with me?

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: There is nothing wrong with you. Some kids are slower to find their people, and that's okay. Want to brainstorm some ideas together?
